title: ranunculus flabellatus var. paludosus
intro: Ranunculus flabellatus var. paludosus, also known as the marsh buttercup, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus flabellatus var. paludosus include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and it can be applied topically to the skin or ingested as a tea to reap its benefits.
